hypothesis: Prediction error magnitude correlates r>0.3 with lesson quality (Sharpe). EAD implements predictive coding: surprise drives learning.

sharpe_analysis.verdict: Weak positive, NOT significant. Ceiling effect at Sharpe 7-9.

citation_analysis.verdict: Null. No relationship between error magnitude and citation impact.

falsified_vs_confirmed.verdict: Sharpe slightly favors falsified (d≈0.4 but underpowered). Citations slightly favor confirmed (d=-0.13). Both NS.

expectations_vs_actual.prediction_verdict: FAILS — both measures bracket zero, well below 0.3 threshold

interpretation.mechanism: EAD works through LOOP CLOSURE (having expect+actual+diff at all → +39.8pp merge, S360), NOT through ERROR MAGNITUDE (how wrong → r≈0). The swarm has a structured-reflection mechanism, not a prediction-error mechanism.
interpretation.brain_analogy: PARTIAL — the swarm has the predict-compare-update loop (structural isomorphism), but lacks error-dependent learning rate (functional divergence). Predictive coding in neuroscience preferentially updates from high-surprise signals; EAD updates uniformly regardless of surprise magnitude.
interpretation.prior_work: S360: loop existence → +39.8pp merge. S367: causal (OR=203). S371: loop closure mediates (phi=0.37). THIS: error magnitude does NOT mediate (r≈0).

verdict: F-BRN2 predictive coding claim REFINED: EAD is attention mechanism (forces reflection), not prediction-error mechanism (doesn't learn differentially from surprises). Brain analogy partial: structure yes, function no.
